rag_rules: Repair AGREE diagnostics by resolving unresolved references to AADL features before modifying contract clauses.
Do not introduce new architecture or bindings to fix type errors; remove invalid clauses if references cannot be resolved.
generic_rules: Fix root unresolved identifiers before addressing type mismatches in contracts.
Ensure all referenced features are visible in the current scope before using them in AGREE expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE scalar types are int, real, and bool — never use 'integer', 'boolean', or other aliases.
Comparison expressions must evaluate to bool; use boolean operators only on compatible types.
anti_patterns: Do not add new subcomponents, connections, or properties to resolve AGREE reference errors — this violates minimal repair.
Avoid assuming package-qualified names or types not declared in the model or its dependencies.
